title: Bug report: under some conditions, 0**0 still gives 0
body:
Hi there!

Using the last version of Verilator (commit fced4d6), I notice that despite the fix of #4721, under some conditions, `0 ** 0` still gives `0`.

In particular, I observed this for the line:
```
assign celloutsig_127z = 12'h000 ** { celloutsig_58z, celloutsig_67z, celloutsig_19z, celloutsig_69z, celloutsig_39z[65] };
```

in the sources that I'm attaching below. My apologies again for the size of the design.
I tested disabling the optimizations one by one and enabling tracing. None of these changes make the problem disappear, so it is not an Verilator optimization problem.
I also added the expected and actual VCD dumps, where you can focus on `celloutsig_127z`.

[Here are the files.](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13686674/0_power_0_again.zip)

Thanks!
Flavien
code_blocks: assign celloutsig_127z = 12'h000 ** { celloutsig_58z, celloutsig_67z, celloutsig_19z, celloutsig_69z, celloutsig_39z[65] };
